brief_justification: Correct answer (Yes) with valid Bayes' theorem calculation yielding ~80.7%; Goes beyond surface by noting causal structure and distinguishing observational vs interventional conditioning; Some self-monitoring via the reasoning context noting not to conflate correlation with causation; Mentions verification need (observational vs interventional) but doesn't verify via alternative derivation; Acknowledges the distinction between observational conditional and interventional claim; Briefly notes alternative causal pathway (wibl→vubr→uvzi) but doesn't systematically evaluate alternatives; Full derivation chain from given probabilities through total probability law to Bayes' theorem is clearly shown and reproducible
